title: Apple Keynote
documentation:
## Purpose
Apple Keynote is a presentation application, and its `.key` / `.keynote` format is a ZIP-based package containing SNAPPY-compressed Protobuf data alongside slide images and other resources. The [Apple Keynote](https://capdag.com/browse/media:keynote) media type represents any Keynote presentation file produced by Keynote for macOS or iOS.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes presentation files originating from the Apple Keynote application — for example, document archiving, slide content extraction, or conversion pipelines that transform Keynote files into PDF or other formats. For cross-platform presentation interchange, PPTX or PDF is preferred.

## Notes
The Keynote format is proprietary and uses the same ZIP + Protobuf structure as Numbers. Implementers that need to extract text or thumbnail images should use AppleScript automation on macOS (to export via Keynote itself) or open-source tools such as `keynote-export`. Direct binary parsing is impractical without reverse-engineered Protobuf schemas. Like Numbers, format detection requires checking ZIP magic bytes and internal path structure.
